mnemonics.title: ER Structure Recall
mnemonics.content: Remember the basic ER structure: 'Every Real Entity Requires Attributes' - E (Entity) R (Rectangle) R (Relationships) A (Attributes) - corresponds to shapes: Rectangle for Entity, Oval for Attribute, Diamond for Relationship

mnemonics.title: Key Keys Remember
mnemonics.content: PRIMARY KEY = 'PK - Pick Key' - underlined in ER diagram. FOREIGN KEY = 'FK - Foreign Kid' - references another entity. Remember: 'PK is unique, FK links you'

mnemonics.title: Weak vs Strong Entity
mnemonics.content: WEAK = 'We Don't Have Own Key' - double rectangle. STRONG = 'Solid Self Sufficient' - single rectangle with its own key. The weak entity depends on strong entity for identification

mnemonics.title: Cardinality Crow's Foot
mnemonics.content: Read from left to right: One (single line), Many (crow's foot/three-pronged). 'Crow flies far = Many' | 'One is alone = single line'

mnemonics.title: Attribute Types
mnemonics.content: SIMPLE = single oval | COMPOSIS = 'COmposite POints Split' - multiple ovals connected | MULTI = double oval | DERIVED = dashed/dotted oval | KEY = underlined oval

mnemonics.title: Participation Total vs Partial
mnemonics.content: TOTAL = double line (total participation) - 'TWO lines for TOTAL' | PARTIAL = single line (partial participation). Remember: 'Double the line, double the commitment (total)'

acronyms.usage: Used to describe the overall diagram and model - fundamental concept in database design representing real-world entities and their relationships
Uniquely identifies each record in an entity; shown as underlined attribute. Every strong entity must have a PK
Attribute that references primary key of another entity; establishes relationship between entities
Defines how many instances of one entity can relate to instances of another entity (1:1, 1:N, M:N)
Total (mandatory) - every entity must participate | Partial (optional) - entity may or may not participate
In cardinality notation (1:N, M:N), N represents 'many' side of relationship - shown as crow's foot symbol
Alternative to N in M:N relationship; both M and N represent 'many' - indicates bidirectional 'many' relationship

visualTips.visualization: Picture entities as NOUNS in a sentence - Person, Student, Course. Rectangles represent 'containers' or 'boxes' holding similar objects. Think of each rectangle as a spreadsheet table
Picture attributes as ADJECTIVES describing entities - Name, Age, Color. Ovals are 'clouds' or 'bubbles' attached to entity boxes. They 'describe' the entity
Picture relationships as VERBS connecting entities - Teaches, Enrolled, Works. Diamond shapes are 'connectors' or 'junction points' between entity boxes
Think of it as a 'dependent' - double rectangle looks like TWO boxes nested, meaning it needs another box (strong entity) to support it
Double oval - think 'double bubble' meaning MORE than one value. Like a student having multiple phone numbers
Dotted/dashed oval - think 'ghost' oval because the value can be CALCULATED or DERIVED from other attributes, not stored
Crow's foot looks like a BIRD'S FOOT - 'birds fly in groups (many)'. Single line is ONE. Place closer to entity to show 'how many' of that entity

analogies.analogy: Think of a CLASS in a school - all students are entities of the 'Student' entity set. Each student is an instance, like each row in a table
Like FAMILY RELATIONSHIPS - Father 'has' Children, Teacher 'teaches' Students. The relationship describes the connection between people
Like AADHAR CARD NUMBER - unique to each person, identifies you uniquely. Every Indian citizen must have one to be identified
Like a CHILD - cannot exist independently, needs parents (strong entity). A 'Dependent' entity in employee database needs 'Employee' entity to exist
Like a FULL NAME - can be split into First Name + Last Name. 'Address' can be split into Street, City, Pincode
Like PHONE NUMBERS - a person can have multiple phones (mobile, home, office). Cannot store single value, need multiple
Like a TEACHER and STUDENTS - one teacher teaches many students. Teacher side is 1, Students side is N (many)
Like STUDENTS and COURSES - one student enrolls in many courses, one course has many students. Both sides have 'many'
Like breathing - you MUST breathe to live. Every entity instance MUST participate. In database: every ORDER must have a CUSTOMER
Like playing sports - you MAY or MAY NOT participate. In database: an EMPLOYEE may or may not manage a DEPARTMENT
